subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B1.1.1.1. Explain who the Creator is
examplars:
1. In groups, learners discuss who the creator is.
2. He is God, the originator of all things.
3. He provides all our needs: air, food, water, life, etc.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B1.1.1.1. Explain who the Creator is
indicator_description: Mention the names of some of the things God created.
examplars:
1. Engage learners with pictures, charts, video clips and songs showing some of the things God created: humankind, animals, trees, rivers, the sea and mountains.
2. Let learners draw, colour and model some of the things God created.
3. Help learners to role play, recite rhymes and sing songs about some of the things God created.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B1.1.1.1. Explain who the Creator is
examplars:
1. In groups, guide learners to talk about the attributes of God.
2. Engage learners with pictures, charts, video clips, songs and role play to demonstrate attributes of God showing: love to one another, truthfulness, kindness, patience, mercy, etc. at home, school and community.
3. Let learners role play the following activities:
4. home: family members caring for one another,
5. school: teachers and learners demonstrate care and love among themselves and
6. community: visits to the hospital and orphanage, etc.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
strand: Religious Practices and their Moral Implications
sub_strand: Religious Worship in the Three Major Religions in Ghana
content_standard: B1.2.1.1. Demonstrate the understanding of religious worship
indicator_description: Name the three main types of worship in Ghana.
examplars:
1. Show pictures, video clips of people worshipping among the three main religions in Ghana.
2. Christian worship,
3. Islamic worship and
4. Traditional worship.
5. Let learners role-play the act of worship in the three main religions in Ghana.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
strand: Religious Practices and their Moral Implications
sub_strand: Religious Worship in the Three Major Religions in Ghana
content_standard: B1.2.1.1. Demonstrate the understanding of religious worship
indicator_description: Recite religious passages and sing religious songs.
examplars:
1. Show pictures, video clips, etc. of songs and recitations from the three main religions.
2. Let learners sing and recite texts from the three main religions:
3. The Lord's Prayer, Psalm 23 (Christian),
4. Al-Fatihah (Islamic) and
5. any recital from the traditional religion-sacred myths, riddle, proverbs, etc. (Traditional).

subject: Religious and Moral Education
strand: Religious Practices and their Moral Implications
sub_strand: Religious Festivals in the Three Major Religions in Ghana
examplars:
1. Guide learners to mention festivals celebrated in the three religions.
2. Christian: Christmas, Easter, etc.
3. Islamic: Eid-ul-Fitr, Eid-ul-Adha, etc.
4. African Traditional Religion (ATR): Odwira, Damba, Homowo, Hogbetsotso, Fetu Afahye, etc.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
sub_strand: Birth of the Leaders of the three Major Religions in Ghana
content_standard: B1.3.1.1. Narrate the birth stories of the leaders of the three main religions
indicator_description: Narrate the stories of the birth of religious leaders.
examplars:
1. Show pictures and video clips depicting the birth of the religious leaders: Christian, Islamic and ATR.
2. Let learners tell stories about the birth of the religious leaders.
3. Let learners dramatise the birth of the religious leaders.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B1.4.1.1. Show an appreciation of the roles of the family and community in their daily lives
indicator_description: Identify the role of the individual members of the family.
examplars:
1. Let learners, in groups, talk about their roles and roles of their parents in the family:
2. Parents: Provision of shelter, food, security and education,
3. Children: running errands, performing house chores, etc.
4. Let learners role-play the functions of the various members in the family, e.g. Learners play the role of a father, mother, etc.

subject: Religious and Moral Education
content_standard: B1.4.1.1. Show an appreciation of the roles of the family and community in their daily lives
indicator_description: Explain the role of the community in the upbringing of its members.
examplars:
1. Let learners, in groups, talk about the role of the community in the upbringing of its members through education, discipline, counselling, etc.
2. Let learners dramatise or role-play the roles of members of the community in the upbringing of its members through education, discipline, counselling, etc.
